component.name: PageHeader
component.description: A slot-based composition shell that defines the top of a page: an optional breadcrumb trail, a real heading with optional right-aligned actions, and an optional bottom bar for search / filters. Purely presentational — it arranges whatever you pass into its slots.
component.note: Flex column, 32px top padding baked in. Title is Heading/L: Inter bold 24/28, neutral.800. breadcrumbs/actions/bottomBar are open ReactNode slots, not fixed layers — pass Breadcrumbs, Buttons, and a TextField. headingLevel (1|2|3) changes the rendered tag for the document outline only; the visual size never changes. Synced to Figma '❖ Page header'.

root.paddingTop: spacing.3xl (32px) — baked in to match the Figma frame

main.layout: flex row, justify-content space-between, align-items flex-start
main.gap: spacing.md (16px) — where the title and actions meet

title.typography: Heading/L — Inter bold, font-size.xl (24px) / line-height.md (28px), font-weight.bold (700)
title.truncate: overflow hidden + text-overflow ellipsis + white-space nowrap (single line); min-width 0 so it can shrink

actions.layout: flex row, align-items flex-start, flex-shrink 0 (right-aligned, never compresses)

useCases: Define the top of a content page — page title plus context and controls
Show a breadcrumb trail above the title for nested navigation
Place page-level actions (Create, Save, Export) to the right of the title
Add a search field and filter controls in a bottom bar above a list or table
Truncate a long, dynamic title to a single line to keep the header height fixed

commonPatterns.name: Title with breadcrumbs and actions
commonPatterns.description: The standard page top: a breadcrumb trail, the title, and one or two primary/secondary actions to the right.

commonPatterns.name: List header with search + filter
commonPatterns.description: A title plus a bottom bar holding a flex-1 search TextField and a Filter button — the toolbar above a table or list.

commonPatterns.name: Truncated title
commonPatterns.description: A long, user-supplied title kept to one line with an ellipsis so the header height stays constant.

commonPatterns.name: Nested-section heading level
commonPatterns.description: Render the title as an h2 to fit a page that already has an h1, without changing its visual size.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using PageHeader as an app-wide top nav or banner
antiPatterns.reason: It is a per-page header (title + page-scoped controls), not a global chrome bar; reusing it as app navigation muddles the document outline and layout.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a dedicated AppBar / top-nav for global chrome; keep PageHeader for the page's own title and actions.

antiPatterns.reason: actions has flex-shrink 0 and sits beside the title; too many buttons crowd the title and break on narrow widths.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep 1–3 primary/secondary buttons inline and overflow the rest into a Menu ("More" / kebab).

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering the title as a plain div or styled span
antiPatterns.reason: Bypassing the heading drops the page's heading semantics and harms screen-reader navigation.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pass the title as children so it renders as a real heading; use headingLevel to fit the outline.

antiPatterns.scenario: Nesting multiple PageHeaders on one page
antiPatterns.reason: A page has one top; stacked PageHeaders duplicate the 32px frame and produce competing top-level headings.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a single PageHeader per page; use FormSection titles or lower headings for sub-sections.

slots.title: Required — the page title, passed as children; rendered as a real heading (Heading/L)
slots.breadcrumbs: Optional — a breadcrumb trail above the title, typically a <Breadcrumbs /> nav landmark
slots.actions: Optional — right-aligned controls beside the title, typically Buttons (icon-only ones need aria-label)
slots.bottomBar: Optional — a full-width row below the title for search / filters (e.g. a flex-1 TextField + a Filter Button)

parentConstraints: Top of a page body / content column
Below a side nav or app shell
Above a list, table, or form

interactions.static: Purely presentational / compositional — no internal state, no event handlers of its own. All interactivity lives in the components passed into the slots.
interactions.truncate: When truncateTitle is set, the title clamps to a single line with an ellipsis (overflow hidden, nowrap); min-width 0 lets it shrink beside the actions.
interactions.responsive: Adapts by letting the title shrink/truncate while actions (flex-shrink 0) stay beside it; the bottomBar stretches full width with a flex-1 search field.

behavior.controlled: Not applicable — PageHeader holds no state. Manage any state (search value, breadcrumb data) in the slotted children.

accessibility.heading: The title renders as a real heading — h1 by default; headingLevel (1|2|3) lets you fit the page's outline without changing the visual size
accessibility.breadcrumbs: The breadcrumbs slot should hold the Breadcrumbs nav landmark (nav with an accessible name) so AT users can skip to it
accessibility.actions: Pass real Buttons into actions; icon-only buttons must carry an aria-label
accessibility.landmarks: PageHeader itself is a plain container; rely on the heading and the breadcrumbs nav landmark for structure
accessibility.contrastNote: neutral.800 title text on the page background meets AA.

athenaConstraints.topPadding: spacing.3xl (32px) — fixed to match the Figma frame
athenaConstraints.titleStyle: Heading/L: Inter bold 24/28, neutral.800 (visual size is independent of headingLevel)

figmaDivergences.figma: fixed Figma layers in the frame
figmaDivergences.reason: Slots let any Button / field arrangement work, instead of hard-coding the Figma layer set.

figmaDivergences.implementation: headingLevel (1|2|3) chooses the rendered tag (h1–h3) for the document outline; visual size unchanged
figmaDivergences.reason: Added so the title can fit any page's heading hierarchy without restyling.
Bakes the Figma frame's top offset into the component so it sits correctly without a wrapper.

aiHints.context: Reach for PageHeader to build the top of a content page: pass the title as children, a <Breadcrumbs /> into breadcrumbs, Buttons into actions, and a search TextField + Filter Button into bottomBar. It is a layout shell with no state — put state in the slotted children. Use headingLevel to fit the page outline and truncateTitle for long dynamic titles.
neverUseWhen: You need an app-wide top nav or global banner — use a dedicated AppBar / top-nav
There are more than ~3 inline actions — overflow the extras into a Menu
You would render the title as a plain div — keep the real heading semantics
A page already has a PageHeader — use one per page, not nested
